interpretation: The audited two-step exact coverage/cardinality propagation gives a large combinatorial partition reduction but does not expose a cheap first/last cube suffix at the unchanged five-second cap. This falsifies the frozen discriminator hypothesis and forbids scaling the 4,402 cubes unchanged.

next_experiment.name: tiny exact-cover ILP/PB forced-substructure discriminator
next_experiment.selection: The lexicographically first depth-two cube in each root_class x sample_category cell: two roots times rank_zero, q3_nonzero, and q4_nonzero.
next_experiment.hypothesis: The remaining difficulty is global exact-20 and overlapping-triple coupling that unit coverage/cardinality propagation cannot see; integer presolve will expose a small common forced or forbidden block set.
next_experiment.method: Build the exact binary covering model for each frozen CNF residual. Use CBC only to propose forced literals or a compact infeasible subconfiguration. Validate every proposed forced literal by solving the opposite exact CNF assumption and requiring an external replayable DRAT proof; validate every claimed subconfiguration with an independent checker. CBC status alone is never a certificate.
next_experiment.success_observable: At least one independently certified forced literal or compact infeasible subconfiguration shared by paired strata, yielding a sound smaller canonical partition or replayable closure path.
next_experiment.failure_observable: No auditable forced literal or compact core in the fixed tiny gate; then abandon propagation-depth scaling and change formalism rather than increasing generic caps.
next_experiment.solver_allocation: Cloud only; local remains structural/checker/publication only while local disk is below the 20-percent free-space gate.

claim_limit: This review gate adds no ordinary-classification closure and no C(12,6,4) theorem progress.
